theme: The one mistake 80% make — flagship carousel

linkedin.text:
After 200 events in 120 countries, here's the one mistake 80% of Israeli companies make on their first incentive trip.

They plan the trip around "fun."

Fun is the cheapest thing money can buy — and the first thing forgotten.

The right way: reverse-engineer the trip from the operational outcome you want 6 months later. Retention. Alliance-building. Leadership exposure. Pick one. Then design backwards.

Fall 2025. A tech company. 130 people. 4 days on a Mediterranean island. Their CFO nearly killed the budget — "we can do dinner in Tel Aviv for a tenth." We rebuilt the brief around one number: keep 40 senior engineers through the Q2 bonus cycle.

7 months later, here's what changed.

The trip's WhatsApp group is still active.
Conversations between R&D and Sales that hadn't happened in 18 months.
Three cross-functional projects started over dinner — two closed in Q4.
Two engineers who'd already accepted external offers withdrew.
The CFO's first words at the post-trip debrief: "I was wrong."

The trip wasn't an HR expense. It was a retention instrument.
You can't put it on a spreadsheet — but the spreadsheet for next year looks very different because of it.

Reply "מספרים" and I'll send the ROI framework we use to build the brief.

Alon Ouaknine
alon@uproduction.co.il

#IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #EmployeeRetention #ROI #IsraeliTech #LeadershipDevelopment #CFO #HRStrategy #EventStrategy

facebook.text:
200 events. 120 countries. One mistake 80% of Israeli companies make.

They plan the incentive trip around "fun" — not around what changes when their team comes back.

Fall 2025: tech company, 130 people, 4 days, Mediterranean island. The CFO nearly killed it. We rebuilt the brief around one question — "How do we get 40 senior engineers to still want to be here in 12 months?"

7 months later: the trip's WhatsApp group is still active. Two engineers withdrew external offers. CFO at debrief: "I was wrong."

The trip isn't an expense. It's an instrument.

DM us for the ROI framework.
